title: Phippsia interior
intro: Phippsia interior, also known as Tibetan club moss or Phippsia, is a rare, alpine plant species belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cold, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Phippsia interior include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Tibetan herbal medicine, Phippsia interior is often prepared as a tea, powder, or capsule, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
